page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8163 processors operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each of the 64 vCPUs maps directly to a physical core, ensuring dedicated compute allocation without resource sharing at the thread level.

The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and data hosting. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps, facilitating reliable data transfer rates for multi-tier architectures.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource density position this instance as a stable platform for workloads requiring predictable compute performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. Appropriate workloads for this hardware profile include enterprise applications, web serving, medium-sized databases, and general-purpose business logic processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, running on the KVM hypervisor. Powered by Intel Xeon 8163 processors at 2.5 GHz with an x86_64 architecture, it provides a dedicated 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, ensuring predictable compute performance without hyperthreading. The instance maintains a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, making it reliant on network-attached storage. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring a stable, non-shared compute allocation. It is well-suited for enterprise applications, web serving, and general-purpose database hosting.
